summary: Comprehensive testing of STRICT STATUS HIERARCHY (Iteration 11). All 16 backend tests passed (100%). All frontend tests passed (100%). The new hierarchy correctly uses email_type field for Shortlisted/Rejected status, with Scheduled as subset of Shortlisted and Attended as subset of Scheduled.

backend: GET /api/status shows correct counts (naukri=10, pipeline=9, registered=9)
GET /api/dashboard-counts: shortlisted=6, rejected=2, scheduled=4, not_scheduled=2, attended=2, not_attended=2
GET /api/role?jobRole=Software Developer: 5 applicants (Alice=Attended, Bob=Not Attended, Dave=Attended, Frank=Not Attended, Henry=Shortlisted)
GET /api/role?jobRole=Data Analyst: 4 applicants (Carol=Shortlisted, Eve=Rejected, Grace=Registered, Ivy Wang=Rejected)
GET /api/summary: Software Developer (total=5, shortlisted=5, rejected=0, scheduled=4, attended=2)
GET /api/summary: Data Analyst (total=4, shortlisted=1, rejected=2, scheduled=0, attended=0)
GET /api/data/rejected: returns 2 records (email_type based, NOT result_status)
frontend: Login page loads with form elements
Dashboard shows DB Status: Naukri=10, Pipeline=9, Registered=9
Roles page shows 2 role cards (Software Developer: 5, Data Analyst: 4)
Software Developer drilldown: 5 applicants with correct status badges
Software Developer drilldown: Attended=green, Not Attended=orange, Shortlisted=amber
Data Analyst drilldown: 4 applicants with correct status badges
Data Analyst drilldown: Shortlisted=amber, Rejected=red, Registered=gray

email_type_based_status.rejected: email_type matches '^reject' (2 records: Eve Brown, Ivy Wang)

seed_data_creation: Test data: 10 Naukri records, 9 Pipeline records. Jack Brown has no pipeline match (unregistered). 9 registered candidates with various email_type values (shortlist, shortlisted, reject, rejected, general).
context_for_next_testing_agent: STRICT HIERARCHY verified working: (1) Shortlisted/Rejected now use email_type field (NOT result_status), (2) Scheduled = subset of Shortlisted (has schedule_date/time), (3) Attended = subset of Scheduled (has otp_verified), (4) derive_status() correctly implements hierarchy, (5) All dashboard counts and data endpoints use email_type-based filtering, (6) Grace Kim with email_type='general' correctly shows as 'Registered' (not counted in shortlisted/rejected), (7) Both 'reject' and 'rejected' email_type values are handled for Rejected status.
